title: Sindhu, Dhruv-Tanisha Advance to Japan Open Second Round
indian_angle: India's top female shuttler PV Sindhu and the mixed doubles pair Dhruv and Tanisha have successfully moved past their opening matches at the Japan Open.
summary: World No. 10 PV Sindhu delivered a strong performance to defeat Wong in straight sets, while the Indian mixed doubles duo of Dhruv and Tanisha also secured their spot in the second round of the Japan Open.

title: BAI Releases Eligibility Criteria for World Junior Championships 2026
indian_angle: The Badminton Association of India has issued crucial guidelines for Indian junior players aspiring to participate in the upcoming World Junior Championships in Cairo.
summary: The Badminton Association of India (BAI) has published the official eligibility criteria for Indian players seeking selection for the BWF World Junior Championships 2026, scheduled to take place in Cairo, Egypt.

title: Delhi World Championships 2026: Attracting Gen Z and Alpha Crucial for Badminton's Future
indian_angle: With the BWF World Championships 2026 set to be hosted in Delhi, there's a focus on engaging younger Indian audiences to ensure the sport's long-term popularity.
summary: An analysis highlights the necessity of captivating Gen Z and Gen Alpha audiences for the success of the upcoming BWF World Championships in Delhi and the overall growth of badminton in India.

title: Indian Shuttlers Need to Develop Independent Thinking, Win Without Coach
indian_angle: An opinion piece discusses the need for Indian badminton players to cultivate self-reliance and tactical acumen on court, reducing dependence on coaches during matches.
summary: An article argues that Indian badminton players would benefit from developing stronger independent decision-making skills during matches, enabling them to secure victories even without direct coaching intervention.

title: New 3x15 Scoring System Implemented in Indian Domestic Tournaments
indian_angle: A significant change in scoring, adopting the 3x15 system, has been introduced for junior domestic ranking tournaments and national championships across India.
summary: The Badminton Association of India has officially implemented the 3x15 scoring system for all domestic ranking tournaments and national championships in the U-13, U-15, and U-17 age groups, aiming to debut it soon.

title: Kidambi Srikanth Finishes as Runner-Up at US Open
indian_angle: Indian star Kidambi Srikanth reached a significant final at the US Open, showcasing his continued form on the international circuit.
summary: Despite a strong run to the final, Indian shuttler Kidambi Srikanth was unable to clinch the US Open title, finishing as the runner-up after a hard-fought match against Su Li Yang.
